title: Lemmaphyllum novoguineense
intro: Lemaphyllum novoguineense, commonly known as New Guinea club moss, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Lycopodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and inflammation, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Lemaphyllum novoguineense include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lemaphyllum novoguineense is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
taxonomy: Lemmaphyllum novoguineense belongs to the family Asparagaceae. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Lemmaphyllum. The plant is native to New Guinea.
